content.text: Design Note - SM-1049: Certificate Module - Oops Page for Login Screen Access
When a user navigates directly to the Certificate Manager URL (e.g.,

) without a valid session — by stripping the UID from a public certificate link or accessing the root URL directly — the app currently shows a login form. Since the certmgr app is only meant to be accessed embedded in the Angular parent app via iframe (sharing session cookies), the login screen should be replaced with a user-friendly "Oops" error page.
— Primary area of change. No backend, database, Angular frontend, or mobile changes required.
(root) — Currently shows LoginForm when unauthenticated; will show OopsPage instead
(all protected routes) — Same behavior; if no session exists after session check, show OopsPage instead of LoginForm
None — no data model changes needed.
session check remains unchanged. The change is purely on the frontend rendering side after the session check completes.
Styled page consistent with the app's Tailwind CSS design
Clear message: e.g., "Oops! This page is not available. Please access the Certificate Manager through the main application."
Navigation button/link pointing to the parent Angular app (e.g.,
May be retained for dev/debug purposes but will no longer be rendered in production flow.
The OopsPage navigation link should be environment-aware (local:
) must remain fully functional and unaffected since it is outside the auth-gated routes.
(404) component for unmatched routes remains separate from the OopsPage — they serve different purposes (invalid route vs. unauthenticated access).
component that displays a user-friendly error page with an AlertTriangle icon, a clear message stating the page should be accessed through the main application, and a "Go to Main Application" button.
to render the Oops page instead of the login form when no valid session exists.
environment variable to all env files for environment-aware navigation back to the parent Angular app.
) remains unaffected and continues to work without authentication.
without a session (use incognito or a new browser) — should see the Oops page instead of the login form
Click the "Go to Main Application" button — should redirect to the main Angular app

Log in through the Angular app and open the Certificates module — should work normally via the iframe session
